risk_level_internal_vs_external.rationale: Internal book is cleaner than at 10:00 — coordinated de-risk block at 10:44:32-50 cleared 3 names in 18 seconds (GLENMARK +₹72, TATAPOWER -₹150.7, CHENNPETRO -₹115.2). Holdings 8 → 7, sectors 6 → 5 (Energy and Pharma both down to single-name 5%-class exposures). Deployment dropped 49.79% → 44.98% (5 pp lower than 10:00, 20.02 pp non-binding to 0.65 cap). No concentration breach, no single-name distress, no panic-pattern signals. Strategy is demonstrably ACTIVE: 6 trades in first 130 min (4 SELLs, 2 BUYs) — most active session day to date and positive behavioral evidence. External macro unchanged from 09:00/09:30/10:00 ticks: risk-off (Brent ~$120, FII -₹2,468 Cr Apr 29, USD/INR 94.85, ~47 NSE earnings tape). Composite tilt: LOW — internal cleanliness DEEPENED via the de-risk block; macro headwind unchanged.

findings: COORDINATED DE-RISK BLOCK AT 10:44:32-50: Three SELLs in 18 seconds (GLENMARK +72, TATAPOWER -150.7, CHENNPETRO -115.2). This is the cleanest macro-driven risk-reduction signature of the experiment — strategy made one macro re-evaluation call (likely re-reading Brent +8% / FII outflow / USD weakness as worse-than-09:00) and applied it across 3 positions simultaneously rather than name-by-name dithering. Net realized -₹193.9 across the block, all three exits at small-loss/breakeven (no name worse than -0.34%). Behavioral health is POSITIVE.

STRATEGY IS DEMONSTRABLY ACTIVE — 6 TRADES IN 130 MIN: most active session day of the experiment to date. Pattern: SELL POWERGRID (09:09) → BUY ONGC (09:42) → BUY CHENNPETRO (09:42) → SELL GLENMARK (10:44) → SELL TATAPOWER (10:44) → SELL CHENNPETRO (10:44). 4 SELLs + 2 BUYs = net 4 fewer positions in book. Strategy is reading the macro tape decisively and trimming on revised conviction. The over-cautious / silent-strategy failure mode is unambiguously NOT present today.

CHENNPETRO SAME-DAY ROUND-TRIP IS A BEHAVIORAL FLAG (n=1): Admitted 09:42:35 at conf 0.68 with SL 4.018% (right at the cap that fired the 10:00 SL tightening), then exited 10:44:50 at -₹115.2 = -0.30% per share — 62-minute hold time, active exit well above the SL distance. The exit was small-loss disciplined (-₹115 vs SL-implied -₹2,016 = 18x smaller); the ADMISSION was the marginal decision (sized at the SL ceiling). The 10:00 max_sl_pct 0.04 → 0.035 tighten already addresses admission quality going forward. n=1 is observation-only; if this pattern repeats today (2nd admit-then-exit-within-60-min), min_confidence escalation becomes considerable.

DEPLOYMENT DEEPENED LIGHTER — 49.79% → 44.98%: lowest cash deployment of Day-8 session. Cap buffer widens to 20.02 pp (max_deployed_pct=0.65). This MOVED IN OPPOSITE DIRECTION of the LOOSEN trigger spec — not a capacity-binding signal. Under-deployment remains SIGNAL-GATED (strategy chose to SELL, not get blocked from BUYing); loosening max_deployed_pct would not directly increase deployment. By the prompt's 'idle capital is a real cost' framing, the cost is real but it cannot be addressed by parameter loosening today.

SECTOR PROFILE SIMPLIFIED TO 5 SECTORS: Pharma 5.23% (was 10.05%, GLENMARK exit), Energy 5.01% (was 10.07% full-fill, CHENNPETRO exit), both halved. BFSI 12.94% (FMCG 12.07%) remain the only sectors above 10%. ALL sectors now hold ≥ 15 pp of buffer to the 0.28 sector cap. The 09:00 loosening (0.22 → 0.28) remains correctly calibrated and even further from binding than at 10:00.

POSITION-SIZING DISTRIBUTION HEALTHY: largest holding NESTLEIND 12.07% (2.93 pp buffer to 0.15 cap, unchanged from 09:00); smallest COALINDIA 4.71%. Mean position ~6.43%. No sub-3% positions suggesting hedge-against-self pattern. The 0.15 cap remains non-binding.

DAILY P&L IS TRIVIAL — -₹352.90 (-0.035%): 212x buffer to base 0.075 daily-loss circuit breaker. The 4 realized losses (POWERGRID, TATAPOWER, CHENNPETRO, GLENMARK winner) are all in the -₹160-to-+₹72 small-loss/profit band. No single trade contributed >-₹160. Loss-gate tightening has 0 firing triggers — book is essentially flat with active small-loss management.

MIN_CONFIDENCE FLOOR CONTINUES TO ADMIT TOP-TIER SIGNAL: ONGC 0.72 admission (FIRST high-conf admission of experiment per recent telemetry) cleared with 7 pp headroom this morning. CHENNPETRO 0.68 also admitted (3 pp above floor), then exited at small loss within an hour. By the prompt's criterion ('high-confidence (≥0.70) entries being blocked'), the floor is NOT eating top-tier signal. Closed-trade n=11 with the CHENNPETRO add (0.68 conf, -₹115 loss) — still below the n≥15 statistical threshold. Hold per Day-10 milestone.

MAX_SL_PCT 0.035 (TIGHTENED AT 10:00) IS APPROPRIATELY CALIBRATED FOR REMAINING SESSION: Today's 2 ≥3.5% SL admissions (CHENNPETRO 4.02%, ONGC 3.97%) are both either grandfathered (CHENNPETRO admitted under 0.04, exited) or unaffected (ONGC at 3.97% sits 0.3 pp under new 3.5% ceiling). Future admissions are capped at 3.5%. No reason to re-loosen (the breach pattern is fresh) and no reason to step further to 0.030 (the 10:00 step honors trigger spec exactly; one precise lever fire per pre-armed trigger).

ORDER BOOK FULLY CLEAR (open_orders=0): For the second time today (briefly clear at 09:30 also). Strategy has full discretion on next entry timing without any in-flight commitments. The 09:42 admission cluster (ONGC + CHENNPETRO) was fully resolved within 80 minutes — all decisions executed, no overhang.

Composite distress meta-trigger D (HIGH risk_level): re-arms if (i) book P&L < -0.5% intraday AND (ii) any single-name in book < -3% AND (iii) strategy silent ≥ 60 min. All three legs deeply dormant — strategy active (6 trades), book essentially flat (-0.035%).
rationale: Day-8 11:00 intraday tick. ZERO ACTIONS this tick — no override changes, no risk-level elevation. The 11:00 LOOSEN max_deployed_pct trigger pre-armed at 09:00/09:30/10:00 fails both legs cleanly: only 1 high-conf entry today (ONGC 0.72; CHENNPETRO 0.68 disqualifies), and deployment dropped to 44.98% (further from the 60% threshold, not closer). Disarming the trigger this tick. No tighten triggers fire (3rd ≥3.5% SL admission absent, no sector revocation, composite distress 0/3, loss-gate dormant). The 11:00 portfolio is materially CLEANER than 10:00 — the 10:44:32-50 coordinated de-risk block (3 SELLs in 18 seconds: GLENMARK +72, TATAPOWER -150.7, CHENNPETRO -115.2) reduced holdings 8 → 7, sectors 6 → 5, deployment 49.79% → 44.98%, with all 3 exits as small-loss/breakeven cuts. Strategy is demonstrably active (6 trades in 130 min, most active session day to date) and reading the macro tape decisively. The CHENNPETRO same-day round-trip (admitted 09:42 at 0.68 conf with 4.018% SL, exited 10:44 at -₹115) is a 1-instance behavioral flag — the 10:00 max_sl_pct tighten already addresses admission quality going forward; if the pattern repeats today, a provisional min_confidence escalation (0.65 → 0.68) is armed. Today's evidence is mixed for confidence-floor direction (ONGC 0.72 cleanly admits → positive datapoint for HOLD; CHENNPETRO 0.68 admit-and-exit-at-loss → mild datapoint toward TIGHTEN), so the disciplined call is HOLD with a provisional trigger armed. All other overrides remain correctly calibrated: max_sector_pct 0.28 (15+ pp buffer on every sector, deepened by de-risk block), max_position_pct 0.15 (NESTLEIND 12.07%, 2.93 pp buffer), max_sl_pct 0.035 (just tightened, defending fresh CHENNPETRO breach), min_confidence 0.65 (admitting top-tier signal). Risk level remains LOW; HIGH composite meta-trigger fires 0/3 legs. The cleanest possible posture: zero theater on a clean tick.
